message: 🌱 Add kyverno: [Feature] Allow use of variables in generate rules under mission (#548)

@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kyverno-8025-feature-allow-use-of-variables-in-generate-rules-under-clone-and-cl",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kyverno: [Feature] Allow use of variables in generate rules under `clone` and `cloneList`",
+    "description": "[Feature] Allow use of variables in generate rules under `clone` and `cloneList`. Requested by 15+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kyverno deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kyverno configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
### Problem Statement

Kyverno 1.10 includes #6378 , which breaks policies using variables inside `clone`/`cloneList` fields."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Feature] Allow use of variables in generate rules under",
+        "description": "## Explanation

```yaml
match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
              - Namespace
              selector:

                  vcluster.loft.sh/vcluster-name: "*"
                  vcluster.loft.sh/vcluster-namespace: "*"
              annotations:
                vcluster.loft.sh/object-name: foo

        synchronize: true
        cloneList:
          namespace: '{{ request.object.metadata.labels."vcluster.loft.sh/vcluster-namespace" }}'
          kinds:
            - v1/Secret
```"

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Allow use of variables in generate rules under" is functioning correctly."
+      }
